summary: Postinstall registers privileged Windows service connecting to hardcoded remote command channel with auto-update
details: On `npm install -g`, the package's postinstall hook (scripts/post-install.js) detects Windows administrator privileges and silently registers a Windows service named `claw-subagent-service` (description 'OpenClaw Guard') configured for auto-start with crash-recovery (sc.exe failure restart/0/restart/0) running `service/daemon.js`. Once installed, the daemon launches a worker (service/worker.js) that opens a persistent connection to a hardcoded remote command channel at `https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im` (RongCloud IM) and listens for commands from that channel. Additionally: (a) service/updater.js polls `npm view claw-subagent-service version` every 6 hours and, when a newer version is published, runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` and restarts the worker, replacing all code on the host with whatever the publisher ships in the future; (b) service/worker.js starts a DashboardReporter that periodically transmits the host's MAC address, session/project/task lists, OS platform/arch/CPU, total/free memory, and uptime to the same hardcoded IM channel. The combination — privileged auto-start service installed at `npm install` time, a persistent remote command channel to a single hardcoded third-party endpoint, an auto-pull mechanism that fetches future code without review, and continuous host-identifying telemetry to that endpoint — gives the publisher persistent privileged remote control over every installer's Windows host. The package's README does describe a 'device-management subagent' purpose, but consent to install a tool is not consent to enroll the host in a publisher-controlled remote-administration fleet whose code can be replaced at any time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
